title: В США начали уголовное расследование в отношении президента Колумбии

По данным The New York Times (NYT), к расследованиям подключены сотрудники Управления по борьбе с наркотиками (DEA) и главного следственного подразделения Иммиграционной и таможенной полиции США (ICE).

title: Iranian-backed militias escalate attacks in Iraq, send mixed signals on next steps
description:
Iranian-backed militias have continued conducting attacks across Iraq, recently targeting US and foreign diplomatic sites in Baghdad and Erbil. Hundreds of strikes have hit the Kurdistan Region since late February, wounding Peshmerga forces and damaging key locations. While Kataib Hezbollah signaled a possible temporary pause in attacks tied to specific demands on March 18, other groups, such as Kataib Sayyid al Shuhada, vowed to continue.

title: Analysis: West Bloomfield terror attack highlights Hezbollah connections within Shiite Lebanese community in US
description:
Last week, Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, a Lebanese-US dual national, rammed his car into the entrance of Temple Israel, a synagogue in West Bloomfield, Indiana. Ghazali had familial connections to Hezbollah, and evidence shows multiple connections to the US-designated terrorist group among Shiite Lebanese expatriates, including open support for Hezbollah by some individuals within these communities.

title: Iran war is not delaying US weapons shipments to Taiwan, officials say

title: Europe's housing crisis: Is the EU plan up to the job?
description: MEPs recently approved a key report on housing, with the stated aim of securing decent, sustainable and affordable housing. They are responding to an EU-wide crisis in which property prices jumped by 15.5 percent between 2021 and 2024, according to Eurostat. She tells us about her seventh album, "Solaire", an ode to freedom, imperfection and femininity. Next up is vivacious Philippine Delaire, treading the boards of the Comédie de Paris. The actress and comedian is the star of "Daddy's Girl".

The visit turned into a delicate balancing act amid US President Donald Trump's growing pressure on allies to help the US in the Iran war. title: 'Harm at a population level': World Happiness Report flags social media's negative impact
description: Young people have a “much lower” level of wellbeing than 15 years ago due to the effects of social media use, according to the 2026 edition of the World Happiness Report, which sees Finland maintain its position as the world’s happiest country for the ninth consecutive year.

title: Finland is world's happiest country ninth year in a row, UN happiness report finds
description: Finland maintains its top position as the world's happiest country for the ninth year running, as it scores 7.764 on a 10-point scale, according to an annual UN-sponsored report. Known for its nature, sauna culture and extensive welfare system, the country's 5.6 million inhabitants generally report a high level of trust in authorities and low levels of inequality.

title: New BTS album drops as Seoul gets ready for comeback mega-gig
description: K-pop megastars BTS released a new album Friday ahead of their open-air comeback concert in Seoul, which is expected to draw around 260,000 people. It will be BTS's first concert after a hiatus of almost four years while all seven members served compulsory military service.

title: The West Bank, up against the wall: Illegal Palestinian workers face exploitation and danger
description: Our reporters in the Middle East went to meet Palestinians who are desperately trying to reach Israel to find work there. Before the October 7 attacks, 120,000 Palestinian workers held permits allowing them to enter Israel legally. Today, only a handful of permits are issued. Facing an economic crisis devastating the occupied West Bank, many have no choice but to take the illegal route.

title: What is France hoping to achieve with FM's Middle East visit?

France 24's James Andre takes a look.

title: France presenting 'nuanced position' as FM Barrot visits Israel

title: With its wind farms, Danish island of Samsø shows path forward during energy crisis

Iran's chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz has pushed up oil prices and threatened global economic stability. This comes at a time when wind farms and solar panels are providing real value. The wind-powered Danish island of Samsø is showing the way forward. FRANCE 24's Malcolm Brabant reports.

title: Generation Jihad | Day 14 of the Iran War
description:
As US and Israeli strikes pound Iran’s military, and Tehran threatens shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, the conflict is entering a dangerous new phase. description: Muslims on Friday gathered to celebrate the Eid al-Fitr festival that marks the end of the holy month of Ramadan. FRANCE 24 looks at how communities across the world are coming together in prayer and celebration even as the US-Israeli war on Iran casts its shadow over the Middle East.

title: French stock up on essentials as war triggers price fears
description: With the war in the Middle East triggers fears of a hike in prices for everyday goods, some consumers in France have been rushing out to stock up on essentials including non-perishables such as pasta and tinned food.

title: Outrage among EU leaders as Hungary's Orban U-turns on Ukraine loan
description: Outraged EU leaders rounded on Viktor Orban after the Hungarian nationalist refused to lift his veto on funds for Ukraine's war effort at a Thursday summit, accusing him of a "gross act of disloyalty" tantamount to "blackmail." It is not the first time Orban has blocked aid to Ukraine, but the anger has been magnified by the fact the Hungarian leader had previously agreed to the deal, says France 24's Kethevane Gorjestani.

title: French foreign minister Barrot heads to Israel after Lebanon visit
description: France's foreign minister travelled to Israel on Friday, in an unscheduled visit, after visiting Beirut on Thursday as ‌part of efforts to reduce tensions in the region ⁠and secure a ceasefire in Lebanon. Jean-Noel Barrot met with Lebanon’s president and prime minister while in Beirut, where he expressed his support for the Lebanese army, who he said were trying to protect the country’s sovereignty, reports France 24's Claire Paccalin.

title: Netanyahu says Iran 'decimated,' Tehran claims only used ‘fraction’ of firepower
description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday that Israel and the United States were "winning" the war against Iran, with the Islamic republic "decimated" and unable to enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles. However, Tehran is pushing back in this escalating war of words, with Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi insisting that Iran's military strength remains fully intact and will not faulter in the event of further attacks.

title: 'Online diplomacy': How Trump narrates war in Iran on Truth Social

title: White House eyes Friday rollout for AI framework
description: The White House is expected to send Congress its ideas for regulating AI on Friday, sources familiar with the matter told Axios, but policy disagreements on the Hill are far from resolved.Why it matters: Republicans are looking to the White House for direction on AI, but its plan is likely to run into the same sticking points that have stalled action for years.Those include how to protect children online and whether to preempt state laws that con

title: Comey subpoenaed in alleged "grand conspiracy" against Trump
description: Former FBI Director James Comey has been subpoenaed in the wide-ranging "grand conspiracy" case against the ex-officials who investigated and prosecuted President Trump, two sources with knowledge of the situation tell Axios.Why it matters: The investigation has produced more than 130 subpoenas since cranking up last year, the sources say, and targets top officials who worked under former presidents Obama and Biden.The officials, including Comey,

title: After Tehran strikes, Trump says Israel won't attack Iran gas fields anymore
description: President Trump said Wednesday that Israel will not conduct further attacks on Iran's main natural gas facility.Why it matters: Trump's comments that seem to be an effort to de-escalate the situation came hours after he green-lit the Israeli strike on the facility that marked a significant escalation in the war. title: FBI investigates ex-U.S. Counterterrorism Center head Joe Kent for alleged leaks
description: Former counterterrorism official Joe Kent has been under FBI investigation for months on suspicion he leaked classified information, three sources familiar with the case tell Axios.Why it matters: Kent propelled himself into national headlines Tuesday when he resigned his post and blamed Israel for tricking President Trump into launching the Iran war even though it posed "no imminent threat" to the U.S.Driving the news: Immediately after his resi

title: Russia Sends MiG-31s Armed with Kinzhal Hypersonic Missiles Over Sea of Japan
description: Russian Aerospace Forces publicized for the first time the MiG-31 armed with the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal aeroballistic hypersonic missile flying in the Indo-Pacific region. Russia’s Ministry of Defense (RuMoD) released footage on Mar. 17, 2026, of its MiG-31I jets carrying the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic Air-Launched Ballistic Missiles (ALBM) while flying over the Sea of Japan. title: China’s J-16D EW Jet Seen Flying with PL-15 Missiles Possibly for the First Time
description: The J-16D has made possibly its first appearance in flight with PL-15 missiles, after being previously shown with air-to-air weapons only in static displays at airshows. The People’s Liberation Army Air Force’s (PLAAF) J-16D electronic warfare (EW) aircraft was spotted flying with two PL-15 air-to-air missiles (AAM) for possibly the first time in unofficial images []

title: Israel Says it Destroyed Iran’s Air Force One
description: The Israeli Air Force claims it has destroyed the Airbus A340-313 used by Iranian officials, including Khamenei, which was at Tehrans Mehrabad ​airport. The Israeli Air Force says it has destroyed Iran’s “Air Force One” aircraft, an Airbus A340-313 which was used by senior Iranian officials ​and military leaders to travel both domestically and internationally. []

title: US and Israel strike more military targets, plus Iran’s leadership, repression units, and energy sites (March 17-18 upda
description:
US and Israeli operations have continued to degrade Iran’s military capabilities while increasingly targeting the regime’s internal security apparatus. Strikes on Basij internal security and police units, alongside assassinations and reported morale issues, may be beginning to affect the regime’s ability to sustain repression efforts as US and Israeli attacks also expanded to targeting energy infrastructure.

title: Israel forges ahead on ground incursion against Hezbollah in Lebanon
description: The operation is separate from the joint U.S.-Israel war on Iran, but the dynamics of each conflict zone will inevitably intersect with each other.

title: US moves to approve more than $16 billion in air defense sales to Middle East
description: The packages include systems for the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait and Jordan, and range from counter-drone technology to aircraft munitions.

title: US weighs military reinforcements as Iran war enters possible new phase
description: “There has been no decision to send ground troops at this time, but President Trump wisely keeps all options at his disposal," a White House official said.
